text.title: Notion PM Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Notion PM workspace must enforce. These are HARD rules — convenience does not override them.

rule.statement: Workspace uses exactly three core databases: Projects (initiatives), Tasks (work items), Sprints (iterations). Relations link Tasks→Projects and Tasks→Sprints bidirectionally; Rollups compute progress without duplicating data.
rule.rationale: Flat databases force manual status tracking; relational + rollups compute project progress, sprint velocity, and workload automatically from task data.

rule.statement: Each database has ≤20 properties. Archive any property not used in a view, rollup, or automation.
rule.rationale: Databases with many properties (especially formula-heavy) load in 3-8s and degrade workspace UX. Lean schemas stay fast.

rule.statement: Use the Status property type (Not Started / In Progress / Complete groups) for task workflow. Select is forbidden for state because it cannot render progress rings in relation views.
rule.rationale: Status is purpose-built for workflow tracking and enables native progress visualisation Select cannot replicate.

rule.statement: Computed aggregates over related rows (count, sum, % complete) use Rollups, never Formulas that iterate prop("relation"). Formulas may post-process a Rollup result but never replace it.
rule.rationale: Formulas that iterate relations are not supported and degrade perf; Rollups are pre-computed and cached.

rule.statement: Done tasks + completed sprints older than 90 days move to an Archive database via linked databases. The live Tasks DB stays under 500 active rows.
rule.rationale: Databases with 500+ active rows and complex properties load slowly; archiving preserves perf without losing history.

rule.statement: API integration caps writes at 3 req/sec per token with exponential backoff on 429. Bulk operations batched; status changes >50 require human checkpoint (no native undo).
rule.rationale: 3 req/sec is the documented per-token limit; silent rate limiting (200 response, queued) leads to stale state. Bulk irreversibility makes the checkpoint mandatory.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
